title: Super funds falling short
full_text:
Australia’s top 30 superannuation funds have invested in just a fraction (4 per cent) of the renewable energy capacity needed to meet Australia’s mandated 2030 climate goals.
Market Forces research finds that since 2020, the top 30 super funds directly contributed $771m to the $99bn invested in Australian clean energy projects – 0.03 per cent of the $2.5 trillion in members’ retirement savings managed by these 30 funds. Only six of our top 30 funds have direct investments in renewable energy or battery storage projects: Aware Super, Cbus, HESTA, NGS Super, Prime Super and Rest.
